title: Live: Trump’s 2026 State of the Union to focus on economy, tariffs and foreign policy
description: President Donald Trump will use Tuesday’s State of the Union address to champion his immigration crackdowns, his slashing of the federal government, his push to preserve widespread tariffs that the Supreme Court just struck down and his ability to direct quick-hit military actions around the world, including in Iran and Venezuela.

title: Iran says deal with US 'within reach' ahead of talks in Geneva
description: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Tuesday a deal to avert military confrontation with the United States was within reach, ahead of talks set to resume in Geneva. His remarks came as Tehran issued a muted warning to student protesters amid mounting US threats over Iran’s nuclear programme.

title: How Russia has finetuned its disinformation campaign in 4 years of Ukraine war
description: Four years into Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, Moscow has redoubled its efforts in disinformation warfare. Its methods have evolved, with more generative AI, more targeting of Ukraine’s allies, and increasing manipulation of real news stories. It’s therefore fitting that disinformation watchdog NewsGuard named a Russian tech bro, Yevgeny Shevchenko, its 2025 “Disinformer of the Year”.

title: South Africa secures release of more nationals lured to Ukraine war
description: In tonight's edition, President Cyril Ramaphosa thanks his counterpart Vladimir Putin for helping to secure the return of 11 South Africans tricked into joining the Russia-Ukraine war. Also, the military spokesperson for the M23 rebel group is reportedly killed in an army drone strike in eastern DR Congo. And more than 6 million people are on the brink of famine in Somalia as the country is facing widespread drought.

title: Russia ‘has not won’ as Ukraine war enters fifth year, Zelenskyy says

title: German Chancellor seeks "right balance" in his first official visit to China

title: 'Ukrainians are getting ready to fight as long as needed', Director of Amnesty Intl Ukraine says
description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Veronika Velch, Director of Amnesty International in Ukraine, says that "Ukrainians are getting ready to fight as long as it would be needed", stressing that "Ukrainians really want peace, but there's very little trust in a peace that will come without justice".

title: Paris says US envoy pledges not to 'interfere' in France affairs

FRANCE 24's August Hakansson reports.

title: Former Iraq PM Maliki vows to pursue return to office despite US opposition
description:
Former Iraqi Prime Minister Nouri al Maliki has vowed to continue pursuing a return to office regardless of US pressure opposing his candidacy, intensifying political uncertainty following Iraq’s November 2025 elections. title: Marking four years of war in Ukraine with powerful music and films
description: In this episode of arts24, we mark four years since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine with powerful films from the frontlines. The BAFTA-winning documentary "Mr. Nobody Against Putin" exposes how Russian schools have been used for propaganda, while Ukrainian filmmaker Mstyslav Chernov captures frontline courage in "2000 Meters to Andriivka", which follows soldiers during the 2023 counteroffensive.

title: Senegal's PM introduces bill doubling prison sentences for same-sex relations
description: Senegalese Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko announced that he had introduced a bill toughening penalties for same-sex relations and making them punishable by five to 10 years in prison amid a wave of homophobia and arrests in the West African country.

title: Ukraine war: Brazil 'seems to want to project an image of not taking sides'
description: While Europe and the US have largely backed Kyiv with sanctions and weapons, countries in the Global South have tried to tow a more neutral line, none more so than Brazil. The country’s president Lula has condemned the invasion in principle, but he also refused to take sides. Our correspondent in Brazil, Jan Onoszko, tells us more.

title: French President Macron accepts the resignation of Louvre director Laurence des Cars
description: The head of Paris's Louvre museum Laurence des Cars on Tuesday handed in her resignation to French President Emmanuel Macron, who accepted it, hailing it as an “act of responsibility”. The museum, shaken by a high-profile heist last October, is in need of a “new impetus”, Macron's office said.

title: Four years of war in Ukraine: An organization turns civilian cars into tactical assets
description: Ukraine marked the fourth anniversary of Russia's full-scale invasion on Tuesday (February 24). For more on that topic, FRANCE 24 spoke to Ivan Oleksii. Witnesses say troops prevented Palestinians from stopping theft of livestock, watched as Nasrallah Abu Siam, 19, was shot and subsequently beaten with clubs while unconscious
The post IDF stood by as settlers killed Palestinian teen, stole hundreds of sheep  report appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: ‘Complete gamechanger’: Iran close to buying supersonic anti‑ship missiles from China
description:
Deal for purchase of CM‑302 missiles, with range of 180 miles, is nearing completion, though delivery date and quantity unclear; expert says they are 'very difficult to intercept'
The post Complete gamechanger: Iran close to buying supersonic anti‑ship missiles from China appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Australia begins probe of antisemitism, security at Bondi event targeted by terrorists
description:
Lawyer says response to evidence requests for Royal Commission 'not presently where we would like it to be'; victims unable to share their accounts due to ongoing legal proceedings
The post Australia begins probe of antisemitism, security at Bondi event targeted by terrorists appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: BAE says its Eurofighter pipeline is filled until first GCAP assembly
description: Europe accounted for 32% of the BAE order book at the end of December, while accounting for 11% of sales in 2025, the company said.

title: Experts worry about nuclear quid pro quo in Russia-North Korea alliance against Ukraine
description: Analysts differ on the question of whether Moscow would trade away highly sensitive nuclear submarine technology in exchange for battlefield support.

title: Estonia begins buying hundreds of pop-up bunkers to fortify border with Russia
description: The Baltic nations are installing a variety of border security features meant to repel an invasion from Russia or Belarus.

title: Four years into Russia's invasion, four young Ukrainians look to the future
description: Their names are Oleksandr, Sofiia, Bohdan, and Angelina. They are between 18 and 28 years old and live in Ukraine. On February 24, 2022, their lives and plans for the future were turned upside down by Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. FRANCE 24 spoke to these young Ukrainians to know more about their hopes and dreams for the future.

title: United States: New York city blanketed in snow
description: New York city remains under a state of emergency after a massive snowstorm pummelled the US northeast on Monday (February 23).

title: Volodymyr Zelensky urges EU to give clear date for Ukraine accession
description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday (February 24) - the fourth anniversary of Russia's invasion -- urged the European Union to set out a clear timeline for his country's accession to the bloc. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg tells us more from Kharkiv.

title: How do US tariffs impact South African economy?
description: Business leaders in South Africa are breathing cautious sighs of relief after the US Supreme Court ruled that some of President Donald Trump’s tariffs were unconstitutional.

title: Russia's 'death economy' and Ukrainian resistance: Four years of war in Ukraine
description: PRESS REVIEW – Tuesday, February 24, 2026: First, the fourth anniversary of the start of the war in Ukraine makes the front pages in France and Europe. Elsewhere, the British front pages discuss the arrest of former British ambassador Peter Mandelson over his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein. Next, the US's hockey teams may have won Olympic gold, but Donald Trump seems to value one more than the other. Finally, Paris's Tour Montparnasse gets a n

title: UK comic Russell Brand pleads not guilty to new rape, sex assault charges
description: Appearing in a London court, Brand denies accusations of raping one woman ​and sexually assaulting another in 2009.

title: When the world retreats: Volunteers are filling Sudan’s humanitarian void
description: Volunteers struggle to fill the gap left by declining international support in Sudan.

title: Ukraine marks four years of Russia’s war
description: It’s been four years since Russia launched its full-scale invasion on Ukraine. Al Jazeera’s Audrey MacAlpine is in Kyiv.

title: European leaders pray with Ukraine’s Zelenskyy on invasion anniversary
description: A number of EU leaders prayed with Ukraine’s President Zelenskyy in Kyiv, to mark Russias invasion anniversary.

title: Airlines Add Flights After Winter Storm and Snow in Northeast Cause Cancellations
description: The major U.S. airlines said they would add more flights in airports that serve cities like Boston, New York and Philadelphia.

title: Israel warns Lebanon it will hit it hard if Hezbollah gets involved in any US-Iran war
description:
Australia begins Royal Commission inquiry into antisemitism after Bondi shooting * US Coast Guard removes swastika from training center, launches inquiry

title: Scoop: Hegseth to meet Anthropic CEO as Pentagon threatens banishment
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has summoned Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei to the Pentagon on Tuesday morning for what sources say is likely to be a tense meeting over terms for military use of Anthropic's Claude."Anthropic knows this is not a get-to-know-you meeting," a senior Defense official told Axios. "This is not a friendly meeting. This is a sh*t-or-get-off-the-pot meeting."Why it matters: Claude is the only AI model available in the military'

title: Report finds geopolitics driving Chinese pivot toward antisemitism
description:
JPPI says Beijing's escalating rivalry with the United States, alignment with Muslim nations, and adoption of anti-Western narratives changing previously positive attitude toward Jews
The post Report finds geopolitics driving Chinese pivot toward antisemitism appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: UK premier visits Poland with family to find wife’s ancestral Jewish home
description:
Victoria Starmer's grandparents fled rising antisemitism before WWI; decades later, the family members who stayed were killed by the Nazis

title: New partnerships enhance Canada’s readiness for future naval projects
description:
Ontario Shipyards and Hanwha Ocean have formalised an MoU aimed at advancing Canada’s capacity to undertake future naval projects.

title: Massive US Air Force warplane movements in Bulgaria raise stakes for Iran talks
description: The U.S. buildup coincides with high-stakes nuclear diplomacy over Iran's nuclear program.

title: Russia-Ukraine four years of war
description: Al Jazeera’s Nils Adler returns to Ukraine to capture how blackouts, air raid sirens and exhaustion shape daily life.

title: Tour Montparnasse Is One of Paris’s Uglier Landmarks. It’s Getting a Makeover.
description: The Tour Montparnasse is one of Paris’s least-loved landmarks. After half a century, it’s finally being remodeled.

title: For Iran’s Rulers, Refusing U.S. Demands Is a Risk Worth Taking
description: The government in Tehran sees capitulating to Washington’s demands on uranium enrichment and ballistic missiles as riskier to its survival than going to war, analysts say.

title: EU Support for Ukraine Stumbles as Hungary Looks to Delay Aid
description: Hungary said that it would block both the latest sanctions package on Russia and a financial aid package to Kyiv worth about $106 billion.

title: The Ukrainian Bureaucrat Working to Squeeze Russia’s War Machine
description: Vladyslav Vlasiuk has spent the past four years pressing Western allies to squeeze the Russian economy through more sanctions. He hopes that 2026 will be the tipping point.

title: Decisive hours: Progress on a new nuclear deal, or countdown to a US strike?
description:
As a condition for continuing talks, the US has demanded that Tehran set out the concessions it is willing to offer, but it's unlikely that the emerging Iranian proposal will meet Trump's threshold requirements. Will he then order a strike, and of what intensity?
The post Decisive hours: Progress on a new nuclear deal, or countdown to a US strike? appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Jewish students warn US Commission on Civil Rights against politicizing campus antisemitism
description:

title: After 4 years of war, Ukraine’s Jews face increasing sirens, shortages and uncertainty
description:
On the grim anniversary of the Russian invasion, those in the Jewish community who haven't fled meet in shelters to light Shabbat candles, while elderly rely on intermittent aid packages

title: Behind the Curtain: AI's biggest threats
description: If AI were a politician, it'd be headed for a landslide defeat.Defeated by Democrats ... Republicans ... and independents.Why it matters: In the Trump administration, Silicon Valley, and select AI-obsessed homes or businesses, people are euphoric about the fast rise of generative AI tools. These groups see a coming utopia.Almost everywhere else, the vast majority are indifferent, pessimistic — even downright dystopian.The politics are shifting so

title: Paris Saint-Germain and Morocco footballer Hakimi to face trial for rape
description: Paris Saint-Germain vice captain Ashraf Hakimi is to go on trial for rape, according to French radio station France Info on Tuesday. Hakimi is also the captain of Morocco's national squad.

title: Reconstruction of Ukraine's economy could cost $588 billion over 10 years
description: Four years to the day after Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, we take a look at the state of the country's economy. The World Bank estimates it will take $588 billion over the next 10 years to make up for damage and destruction incurred during the conflict. The housing, transport and energy sectors have seen the biggest losses. Also in this edition: Panama takes control of two key ports on the Panama Canal.

title: Ukraine marks four years since Russia's full-scale invasion
description: Ukraine marked the fourth anniversary of Russia's full-scale invasion on Tuesday (February 24). Tens of thousands of lives have been lost since the Kremlin ordered troops into Ukraine on February 24, 2022.

title: No end in sight for displaced Ukrainians after four years of war
description: This Tuesday, February 24 marks four years since Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, forcing millions to flee their homes. Today there are an estimated four million Internally Displaced Persons (IDPs) in Ukraine. As the front line moves, endangering areas that were previously considered relatively safe, the shortage of suitable long-term accommodation options for these IDPs remains chronic. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg, Illia Dyadik
